risks.risk: AI Model Accuracy Below User Expectations
risks.description: The AI prioritization model may not achieve sufficient accuracy in real-world usage, leading to user frustration and low adoption of the core differentiating feature.
risks.mitigation: Implement progressive trust-building: start with suggestions, allow easy manual override, collect feedback to improve model. Provide transparent explanations for every priority score. Set launch bar at 75% user agreement rate rather than absolute accuracy.
risks.contingency: If accuracy stays below 60% agreement rate at beta, pivot to rule-based prioritization with AI as an optional enhancement layer.
triggers: User satisfaction survey shows < 70% agreement with AI suggestions
Support tickets related to AI accuracy exceed 10% of total

risks.description: External services (Slack, Google Calendar, Jira, GitHub) may introduce breaking API changes that disrupt the integration platform, affecting users who depend on data sync.
risks.mitigation: Pin API versions, implement adapter pattern for each integration, add health monitoring with automated alerts on sync failures, maintain a 2-version backward compatibility window.
risks.contingency: Gracefully degrade: disable affected integration with user notification, queue sync data for retry when API is stable.
triggers: Integration health monitoring detects API error rate > 5%
Slack/Google Calendar/Jira/GitHub announces breaking API changes

risks.description: Major task management platforms (Asana, Monday.com, ClickUp) may rapidly add AI prioritization features, reducing TaskFlow Pro's competitive differentiation window.
risks.mitigation: Speed to market with deep AI experience (not surface features). Build switching costs through personalized models that improve with usage. Focus on Focus Mode as a secondary differentiator that competitors are unlikely to prioritize.
risks.contingency: If a major competitor launches comparable AI features before our GA, pivot marketing to emphasize Focus Mode and privacy-first AI approach as differentiators.
triggers: Asana, Monday.com, or ClickUp announces AI prioritization features
TaskFlow Pro user churn rate increases month-over-month

risks.description: Handling user task data with AI processing may create compliance challenges with GDPR, CCPA, and industry-specific regulations, especially for enterprise customers in healthcare or finance.
risks.mitigation: Implement privacy-by-design: data minimization, user consent flows, data residency options, and comprehensive audit logging. Engage compliance consultant for GDPR/CCPA review.
risks.contingency: If compliance issues cannot be resolved, offer on-premise deployment option for regulated industries with longer sales cycles.
triggers: Enterprise deal blocked by compliance review
Legal team identifies new regulatory requirements

risks.description: The AI model requires large volumes of representative task data to train effectively. Synthetic data can bootstrap initial development but production-quality model requires real user data with appropriate consent.
risks.mitigation: Start with synthetic data generation using patterns from public task management datasets. Design onboarding flow to encourage task creation. Implement feedback loops to continuously improve model with real data.
risks.contingency: Extend beta period with synthetic data while building user base. Partner with early design partners who can provide anonymized historical task data.
triggers: Beta user base < 500 active users by month 3
Model accuracy plateaus below 70% on synthetic validation set

assumptions.assumption: Users will provide explicit consent for AI processing of their task data
assumptions.rationale: AI personalization requires access to task content and patterns
assumptions.riskIfFalse: Would require pivoting to non-AI features or explicit consent-plus pricing model

assumptions.assumption: AI prioritization accuracy above 75% is achievable with available training data
assumptions.rationale: Based on benchmarks from similar task management AI projects
assumptions.riskIfFalse: Would require rebalancing investment toward rule-based features

dependencies.name: Slack API Access
dependencies.description: Slack integration for notifications and task sync
dependencies.risk: API deprecation or breaking changes without sufficient notice
dependencies.mitigation: Version pinning and adapter pattern implementation

dependencies.name: OpenAI/Anthropic API
dependencies.description: LLM APIs for AI prioritization analysis
dependencies.risk: Price increases, service degradation, or provider exit

dependencies.name: Training Data Pipeline
dependencies.description: Pipeline for anonymizing and feeding user task data into model training
dependencies.risk: Data quality or consent issues prevent effective training
dependencies.mitigation: Synthetic data bridge + explicit consent framework
